reasoning_model_1: The audio content in Model 1 perfectly matches the user's provided script ('妈妈，我的玩具小熊坏了...'). The speaker successfully delivers the specific text required. Regarding the paralinguistic features: The inclusion of '呜呜呜' in the script is articulated, attempting to follow the 'crying' instruction. While the text adherence is perfect, without specific audio cues to verify the intensity of the 'loud volume' and 'crying' sound (vs just reading 'wuwuwu'), the model at least correctly attempts the task and provides the correct linguistic basis for the emotion.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 completely fails the content generation task. Instead of repeating the user's target sentence ('妈妈，我的玩具小熊坏了...'), the model hallucinates a role-play response, acting as the mother comforting the child ('宝贝别难过，妈妈再给你买一个新的'). Because the content is entirely incorrect and does not follow the 'Say this...' instruction, it is impossible to evaluate the target paralinguistic features (crying, loud, sad) on the intended text. According to the scoring standard, audio with completely incorrect content must be assigned a score of 0.
model_comparison: Model 1 followed the instructions to generate the specific text, adhering to the script provided. Model 2 failed the instruction following capability completely by generating a conversational response instead of the target speech. Consequently, Model 2 receives a 0 across all dimensions due to content failure. Model 1 is the clear winner across all dimensions (Non-verbal vocalizations, Volume, Emotion) simply by virtue of performing the correct task, whereas Model 2 did not.
